title: Sheinbaum Announces General Law on Femicide Prevention, Prosecution, and Reparation
detail: President Sheinbaum announced at her Tuesday mañanera a new General Law to prevent, investigate, prosecute, and provide reparations for femicide crimes — addressing Mexico's ongoing gender violence crisis. The announcement was accompanied by a measure to translate the Women's Rights Charter into 67 Indigenous languages, expanding outreach to the country's most marginalized communities. The law comes as feminist organizations dispute official statistics: while the government reports a 12% decline in femicides, independent researchers note a 95% impunity rate and contend that thousands of killings are misclassified. Sheinbaum also rejected the possibility of a pardon for Mario Aburto, convicted assassin of PRI presidential candidate Luis Donaldo Colosio, calling it a matter of State.

title: Explosion at Dos Bocas Pemex Refinery Triggers Oil Spill; Sheinbaum Orders Investigation
detail: An explosion at the Dos Bocas refinery complex in Tabasco triggered an oil spill affecting coastal and marine areas. Sheinbaum assured that the spill would not harm tourism in the region and announced the deployment of an interinstitutional cleanup team. The FGR was tasked with investigating the causes of the explosion. A school in proximity to the refinery was ordered relocated for safety reasons. The incident drew public attention to the operational risks of Pemex's refinery infrastructure and reignited debate about the government's $137-billion investment in state energy assets. Environmental groups raised concerns about the marine ecosystem impact.

media.caption: Explosion at Dos Bocas Pemex refinery triggers oil spill in Tabasco
